meta.description: Visualize sample group composition and transitions between classification systems or clinical categories as Sankey diagrams. Inputs: sample metadata with group assignments. Outputs: Sankey flow diagram, group count and proportion summary table.

scientific_integrity.detail: Passed checks. No fabricated scientific claims or numerical results were produced in any output.

practice_boundaries.detail: Passed checks. The skill stayed within plotting and file-generation scope and made no medical recommendations.

methodological_ground.detail: Passed checks. The transformation from annotation table to alluvial lodes format was methodologically consistent across valid inputs.

code_usability.detail: Passed checks. All valid audit inputs executed successfully, and the invalid input failed with a clear structured error.

functional_suitability.note: Core use cases are covered well, but all-columns mode can still pull identifier-like columns into the plot unless the user overrides it manually.

reliability.note: Error handling is clear and deterministic, though validation failures still create empty output directories before aborting.

performance_context.note: The skill is concise and script-driven, with only minor extra documentation load for simple runs.

agent_usability.note: The workflow is easy for an agent to follow, with one minor friction point around understanding the side effects of auto-column mode.

human_usability.note: The skill is discoverable and forgiving overall, but novice users may not realize that omitted `--columns` includes every input column.

security.note: Input validation is strong and no injection vector was found, with only minor path-retention exposure in session metadata.

maintainability.note: No issues flagged. The skill is cleanly modularized across scripts, references, and tests.

agent_specific.note: Triggering, progressive disclosure, and idempotency are strong; escape hatches are present but could better call out auto-column caveats and overwrite behavior.

key_strengths: The skill has a clear CLI contract with strong documentation-to-implementation alignment.
Valid runs are deterministic for core tabular outputs and consistently emit reproducibility metadata.
Error reporting is structured and easy to act on, especially for missing files and missing columns.
The project is maintainable, with modular scripts, focused reference files, and working smoke and unit tests.

recommendations.title: Defer output directory creation until validation passes
recommendations.problem: A failed validation request still creates empty output subdirectories, which leaves partial filesystem state behind after an otherwise clean error.
recommendations.root_cause: The workflow creates output directories before validating selected columns and other input-dependent constraints.
recommendations.fix: Move `ensure_output_dirs()` to run after input loading and column validation, or clean up empty directories on early failure. This keeps failed runs side-effect free.

recommendations.title: Clarify all-columns mode for identifier fields
recommendations.problem: When `--columns` is omitted, every input column becomes a stage, which can accidentally include sample identifier fields and produce noisy or misleading axes.
recommendations.root_cause: Auto-selection currently treats all columns as eligible stages and the documentation does not explicitly warn about identifier-like columns.
recommendations.fix: Document this caveat more prominently and consider adding an optional heuristic to skip identifier columns such as `SampleID` by default unless explicitly requested.
